creator: scheidell@FreeBSD.org
text:
A suggestion:
if you are patching two or more  different ports, put each port in a 
different attachment.
As it is, we have to split your patch into multiple files (sometimes)

or:
One more thing you might do, that would help everyone AND you, (and you 
can put your patch in one attachment)

use the new subversion ports tree.

sync up tree with svn (or just your ports), do your patch, and then so a 
'svn diff'.
This creates a diff with an Index, and we can patch multiple ports with 
'patch -EunI' (the -I looks for the Index line in diff)

-- 
Michael Scheidell, CTO
 >*| * SECNAP Network Security Corporation
d: +1.561.948.2259
w: http://people.freebsd.org/~scheidell
